revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本古墳大辞典]]
'''Nihon Kofun Daijiten''' (日本古墳大辞典, "Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofuns") is an encyclopedia on kofun (ancient Kofuns) published by [[Tokyodo Shuppan]] in {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}} (Heisei 1). This article also covers the sequel, '''''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten''''' (続 日本古墳大辞典), published in {{ill|2002|en|2002|qid=Q1987|lt=2002|1=2002}} (Heisei 14). == Nihon Kofun Daijiten == The ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' is a revised and expanded version of the ''Kofun Jiten'' proposed in {{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=1982|1=1982}} (Showa 57) by [[Hatsushige Ōtsuka]] and [[Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|Saburō Kobayashi]]. It was edited by Ōtsuka, Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano, and features important kofun from prefectures throughout Japan<ref>Kofun in Hokkaido are not included.</ref>. The compilation involved 118 contributors and provides descriptions of 2,800 entries and 4,000 kofun<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokyodoshuppan.com/book/b79303.html |title=Nihon Kofun Daijiten |publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan |date=2014-10-28 |accessdate=2015-02-27|qq=}}</ref>. The first edition was published on September 19, 1989, with 644 pages. Each entry generally includes the following details: * Pronunciation of the kofun name * Location * Year and personnel involved in investigations * Shape and dimensions of the kofun * External and internal structures * Types and quantities of burial goods * Notable features and period estimation * Status of historical designation (national or prefectural) * Custodian of excavated artifacts * Key references == Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten == The ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Sequel) was edited by Ōtsuka and Kobayashi, with contributions from 295 individuals. It was first published on September 24, 2002, with 530 pages. Based on excavation surveys conducted after the publication of the original ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten,'' this volume covers 1,800 entries and 2,000 kofun. Some kofun featured in the earlier volume that were re-investigated are also included<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokyodoshuppan.com/book/b79603.html |title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten |publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan |date=2014-10-28 |accessdate=2015-02-27|qq=}}</ref>. Additionally, this sequel incorporates entries on {{ill|Yokoanabo|ja|横穴墓|lt=yokoana-bo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber tombs), kilns associated with kofun construction, and ritual sites. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Book-stub|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|日本古墳大辞典|version=99097086|comment=Imported full ja history}}
